subject: From bowel to behaviour - choice articles from F1000
body:
To ensure you receive emails from Faculty of 1000, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.

-----------------------------------------------------

Faculty of 1000 |  http://f1000.com                
Monthly Update  |  February 2014 

-----------------------------------------------------
 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
 Top 10 articles published in 2011-13    
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
 
We've compiled a list of the most influential biomedical articles of the last 3 years according to their F1000Prime score. Read the F1000 blog [ http://blog.f1000.com/2014/01/15/most-influential-biomedical-articles-2011-13/ ] for a summary of each article and a free-to-access link to the article recommendations in F1000Prime.  
 

   F1000Prime                             
==================

1,073 recommendations  [ http://bit.ly/LP64sl ] added in the past 30 days*. Below is a selection:  

>From bowel to behaviour   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718201192?key=1F8E2Tef90qzoSh ]   
This study provides a link between maternal immune activation, offspring gut microbiota and autistic behaviour, providing support for the "psychobiotics" concept.   
Hsiao EY, McBride SW, Hsien S et al.  
Cell. 2013; 155:1451-63  

More than one way to regenerate    
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718185727?key=Dp6RtmaKnQGHIks ]   
The view that limb regeneration in salamanders occurs via mature muscle cells de-differentiating into progenitors is challenged by the discovery that muscle stem cells can also contribute to regeneration.   
Sandoval-Guzmán T, Wang H, Khattak S et al.   
Cell Stem Cell. 2013; Epub ahead of print   

Ultrafast endocytosis   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718197285?key=yyrKJCthVIRRQ3D ]   
This may be the final point to 40 years of hot debate over the mechanism by which synaptic vesicles are recycled at the synapse. It seems that endocytosis occurs much faster than expected.   
Watanabe S, Rost BR, Camacho-Pérez M et al.   
Nature. 2013; 504:242-7   

*Accurate at the time of writing.

 F1000Prime Reports 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 

Open-access review journal [ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/ ], reporting on the hottest topics.   

Proteostasis and longevity: when does aging really begin?    
[ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/b/6/07 ]   
Changes in the proteostasis network, which consists of molecular chaperones and stress-response transcription factors, may be one of the earliest events dictating healthy aging.
F1000Prime Report by John Labbadia and Richard I. Morimoto [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/1759598576295980 ].   
F1000Prime Rep 2014, 6:7   

 ---------------
 See all recent F1000Prime Reports          
 >> http://f1000.com/prime/reports/                 
 ---------------   

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 Getting started with F1000Journal Clubs     
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

We've put together a series of posters to help raise awareness of journal club meetings organized with our new tool. Pick your favorite here [ http://blog.f1000.com/2014/02/04/getting-started-with-f1000journal-clubs/ ].
 
    

   F1000Research                      
======================

Our innovative open access journal [ http://f1000research.com/ ] offering rapid publication, open peer review and open data.

Below is a selection of our latest peer reviewed articles:  


RESEARCH ARTICLE   
JSim, an open-source modeling system for data analysis [ http://f1000research.com/articles/2-288/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/2fk ]   
Latest Published: 30 Dec 2013   
Butterworth E, Jardine BE, Raymond GM et al.   
F1000Research 2:288   

CORRESPONDENCE   
Risks of testosterone therapy in elderly men [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-11/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/2oc ]   
Published: 15 Jan 2014   
Ramasamy R, Dupree JM, Kovac JR et al.   
F1000Research 3:11   

COMMENTARY   
Alternative ideas to increase the percentage of filled seats in nephrology fellowships [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-3/v2 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/2sa ]    
Published: 09 Dec 2013   
Desai T.   
F1000Research 3:3   

~~~~~~~~~
Science Communication Articles    
~~~~~~~~~

F1000Research now accepts articles about science communication. This includes research or opinion articles on topics such as science education, outreach, policy, data sharing, or publishing. Throughout 2014, we are waiving the article processing charge [ http://f1000research.com/sci-comm2014 ] on these papers.

~~~~~~~~~
Rare Disease Day   
~~~~~~~~~

Are you involved in rare disease research or patient care? Please see our blog [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/02/04/publish-rare-disease-research-in-f1000research-for-free ] for our plans for Rare Disease Day.


 ---------------------------------------------
  Submit now to F1000Research          
  >> http://f1000research.com/author/submit        
 ----------------------------------------------
 

    
    F1000Posters                 
======================
 
Open Repository [ http://f1000.com/posters ] for posters and slide presentations; completely free to view and submit. Below are a few selections:

* SCIENCE COMMUNICATION *

Distribution and citation impact of industry-authored articles in medical journals (2008-12)  
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1094943 ]
Spray I, Floch E.
F1000Posters 2014,
5:63  


* DERMATOLOGY *
Breaking the mold! Tackling post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation in acne with concomitant use of oral isotretinoin and chemical peels: not that risky after all!   
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1094973 ]
Sonthalia S, Sarkar R, Bharti J.
F1000Posters 2013,
5:71

 ----------------------------------------------
  Deposit now        
  >> http://f1000.com/posters/fordepositors      
 ---------------------------------------------- 


  
----------------------

 * F1000Tips *

Follow @F1000Tips on Twitter [ http://twitter.com/f1000tips ] for tips, tricks, and customer support for all Faculty of 1000 service.

---------------------- 

-----------------------------------------------------   

In addition to this Monthly Update, we also send out emails featuring deals and discounts for registered users. To sign up, registered users should visit MyF1000 via any F1000 product and opt-in to receive "Information from F1000 about special offers" (under Email Alerts).

You have received this message because you asked to receive the F1000 Monthly Update to your registered email address from Faculty of 1000. If you do not wish to receive any further such messages, you may unsubscribe [ http://www.f1000.com/unsubscribe/email?email=jeeproject@yahoo.com&emailAlertType=MONTHLY_UPDATES ] at any time. For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

Faculty of 1000 Ltd | Science Navigation Group  
Registered in England and Wales with Company Number 3739756    
Middlesex House, 34-42 Cleveland Street | London, W1T 4LB | United Kingdom
